article_meta.title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe

reasoning: The model repeatedly violated the attribution rule by flagging quoted statements as author bias and included at least one questionable slant finding and a misapplied label. Overall, multiple false positives and attribution errors; no major missed biases.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged two highly prominent direct quotes from City Attorney Chiu as author bias, explicitly ignoring the rule despite acknowledging they were quotes.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Two items are pure attribution violations; the fourth mislabels a neutral/attributed aside as slant; only the third item (slant) is debatably valid.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Article’s author prose is largely neutral and attributed; no clear missed author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Misapplied 'Slant' to an asserted linkage that, if biased, would be closer to opinion-presented-as-fact; the two quoted-spin items are inapplicable due to attribution.
